title: The One in Barbados (Part 1)

jokes.text: That's right. Daddy and Uncle Joey are going on a trip today.
jokes.setup: Ross talking to his baby about going on a trip
jokes.punchline: Referring to Joey as 'Uncle Joey' to a baby creates an oddly domestic scene
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor establishing Ross as the dad and Joey as the fun uncle figure

jokes.text: These babies will get you into all the paleontology lectures and seminars.
jokes.setup: Ross proudly announcing conference passes
jokes.punchline: His enthusiasm about paleontology lectures shows his nerdy character
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor showing Ross's obliviousness to others' lack of interest in his field

jokes.text: Do you have anything that will get us out of them?
jokes.setup: Ross offering passes to paleontology lectures as a treat
jokes.punchline: Chandler's immediate desire to avoid them
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline structure with excellent economy. Classic Chandler sarcasm that's both character-specific and relatable

jokes.text: He's been in Minsk for eight years. If he gets too much direct sunlight.. he'll die.
jokes.setup: Explaining why David would want to attend lectures
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation from pale scientist to literally dying from sunlight
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation that's very Phoebe-specific. The vampire-like description is both unexpected and memorable

jokes.text: It's a blanket, Joe, not a cloak of invisibility.
jokes.setup: Joey's plan to get under a blanket on the plane with Charlie
jokes.punchline: Chandler's reality check about airplane blankets not providing privacy
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor with a great nerdy reference. Perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: I know. There are gonna be some pasty folks by the pool tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Looking at all the paleontologists at the conference
jokes.punchline: Observation about scientists being pale/pasty
jokes.explanation: Straightforward observational humor about scientist stereotypes

jokes.text: I think I've been recognized.
jokes.setup: Someone approaching Ross excitedly
jokes.punchline: Ross assumes it's celebrity recognition when it's actually academic recognition
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ross's ego and misunderstanding of the situation

jokes.text: That never happens.
jokes.setup: Ross saying 'This happens all the time' about being recognized
jokes.punchline: Charlie's immediate contradiction
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and economy deflating Ross's ego. Great reaction beat

jokes.text: Sarah. I dig you.
jokes.setup: Ross signing an autograph for a paleontology fan
jokes.punchline: Using 'dig' which works both as slang and paleontology reference
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's perfectly Ross - using paleontology terminology in casual conversation

jokes.text: You probably recognize me from a little show called The Days of Our Lives.
jokes.setup: Joey introducing himself to a paleontologist
jokes.punchline: Adding 'The' to the show title, making it sound more prestigious
jokes.explanation: Character-specific humor showing Joey's tendency to embellish his importance

jokes.text: Dude, it's just Days of Our Lives. There's no 'the.'
jokes.setup: Joey incorrectly naming his show
jokes.punchline: Charlie's correction deflating his attempt at grandeur
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of Joey's pretension with sharp correction

jokes.text: What's all your furniture pointed at?
jokes.setup: Sarah saying she doesn't own a TV
jokes.punchline: Joey's confused question about furniture arrangement without a TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observational humor that became a cultural touchstone. Perfect Joey logic and incredibly quotable

jokes.text: Well, didn't you two come to the right convention.
jokes.setup: Charlie and Rachel admitting they don't have TVs either
jokes.punchline: Ross's sarcastic observation about TV-less people at a paleontology conference
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation connecting the no-TV theme to the nerdy conference setting

jokes.text: Yeah. That's the same as, 'It has something to do with wind.'
jokes.setup: David giving a complex scientific explanation of how planes fly
jokes.punchline: Chandler reducing it to something simple about wind
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of scientific pretension with relatable everyman perspective. Very Chandler

jokes.text: Mike? Who's Mike?
jokes.setup: Phoebe mentioning Mike casually
jokes.punchline: David's confusion about who Mike is
jokes.explanation: Sets up the awkward Mike/David situation, more plot setup than pure comedy

jokes.text: That's a blast from the past, huh?
jokes.setup: Phoebe realizing she mentioned her ex-boyfriend Mike
jokes.punchline: Her awkward attempt to downplay it
jokes.explanation: Awkward recovery attempt that builds cringe tension

jokes.text: Monica refers to Chandler as Richard all the time.
jokes.setup: Trying to make David feel better about the Mike mistake
jokes.punchline: Making it worse by bringing up Monica's ex situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness by Phoebe making the situation worse while trying to help

jokes.text: Well, at least you took me down with you.
jokes.setup: Phoebe accidentally revealing Monica's ex issue
jokes.punchline: Monica's resigned acceptance of being dragged into the mess
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the escalating awkwardness, showing Monica's exasperation

jokes.text: Your feelings for Chandler are certainly gone.
jokes.setup: Monica doing it again - calling Chandler 'Richard'
jokes.punchline: Having to correct herself again
jokes.explanation: Running gag escalation showing the name confusion is a real problem

jokes.text: See, if you want advice, go to Ross or Monica. Or Joey.. for advice about pizza toppings or a burning sensation when you pee.
jokes.setup: Chandler saying he's not good at giving advice
jokes.punchline: Limiting Joey's expertise to pizza and STD symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Joey's limited areas of expertise, with nice escalation

jokes.text: Why did Phoebe and Mike break up? - Because his penis was too big.
jokes.setup: David asking a serious question about the breakup
jokes.punchline: Chandler's inappropriate joke response
jokes.explanation: Very Chandler response - inappropriate joke when asked for serious advice. Good character consistency

jokes.text: I'm sorry. That's the kind of thing I do.
jokes.setup: Making an inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: Chandler's self-aware acknowledgment of his behavior
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about Chandler's character trait of making inappropriate jokes

jokes.text: Well, we probably won't invite you to the wedding, but..
jokes.setup: David planning to propose and beat Mike
jokes.punchline: Already planning Mike's exclusion from the wedding
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing David getting carried away with the wedding planning before even proposing

jokes.text: I didn't mean a thong. I meant thongs.
jokes.setup: Charlie expecting Joey to wear a thong
jokes.punchline: Joey clarifying he meant flip-flops
jokes.explanation: Simple misdirection joke based on word confusion

jokes.text: You really should have been more clear about that.
jokes.setup: Joey's clarification about thongs vs thong
jokes.punchline: Charlie's dry reaction implying she was disappointed
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan reaction extending the thong joke

jokes.text: Britney Spears! Yeah. She never misses these conferences.
jokes.setup: Ross asking Joey to guess who he saw
jokes.punchline: Joey's absurd suggestion that Britney Spears attends paleontology conferences
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist Joey logic connecting pop star to academic conference

jokes.text: You could say, 'Hey, Kenny, how come you're not Britney Spears?'
jokes.setup: Ross asking what to say to Kenneth Schwartz
jokes.punchline: Joey's suggestion continuing the Britney theme
jokes.explanation: Callback to the Britney joke with Joey's characteristic illogical suggestion

jokes.text: You got yourself a very weird deal.
jokes.setup: Joey and Charlie agreeing to both wear thongs for dinner
jokes.punchline: Charlie's observation about the strangeness of their agreement
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat acknowledging the absurdity of the thong deal

jokes.text: I wasn't gonna swim. I was gonna dig a hole.
jokes.setup: Suggestion about using the indoor pool
jokes.punchline: Joey's random plan to dig a hole instead
jokes.explanation: Random Joey logic showing his simple approach to entertainment

jokes.text: you are pink with barely controlled glee.
jokes.setup: Monica being excited about something
jokes.punchline: Ross's vivid description of Monica's obvious excitement
jokes.explanation: Excellent descriptive phrase that's both specific and universally relatable

jokes.text: A penis one!
jokes.setup: Monica asking if he made an inappropriate joke
jokes.punchline: Chandler proudly confirming he made a penis joke
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier inappropriate joke with Chandler's pride in his immaturity

jokes.text: Everything is upside-down here. It rains all day long and nobody watches TV, and Ross is famous.
jokes.setup: Joey describing what's wrong with Barbados
jokes.punchline: Escalating to Ross being famous as equally bizarre
jokes.explanation: Great escalation putting Ross's fame in context with other bizarre occurrences. Very Joey perspective

jokes.text: Monica's hair is twice as big as it was when we landed.
jokes.setup: Describing the humidity effects
jokes.punchline: Monica's hair expanding dramatically in the humidity
jokes.explanation: Visual gag that will pay off throughout episode with Monica's hair getting bigger

jokes.text: That's why our honeymoon photos look like me and Diana Ross.
jokes.setup: Explaining her humidity hair problems
jokes.punchline: Comparing herself to Diana Ross's iconic big hair
jokes.explanation: Great visual comparison that's both specific and relatable to anyone with humidity hair issues

jokes.text: I was just lusting after Chandler.
jokes.setup: Monica clarifying which guy Rachel might want to sleep with
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sarcastic claim about lusting after Chandler
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic deflection of the awkward situation about sleeping arrangements

jokes.text: That's one-seventieth of a carat. And the clarity is quite poor.
jokes.setup: David showing off his engagement ring
jokes.punchline: The ring being pathetically small and low quality
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing David's scientist precision about his inadequate ring

jokes.text: Are you leaving the Supremes?
jokes.setup: Monica wanting to talk to Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Phoebe referencing Monica's Diana Ross hair
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback extending the Diana Ross comparison with perfect Phoebe delivery

jokes.text: I can just see it. 'Mike, do you take Phoebe?' You know? It's every girl's dream.
jokes.setup: Phoebe describing forcing Mike to marry her
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about it being 'every girl's dream'
jokes.explanation: Good sarcastic observation about forced marriage scenarios

jokes.text: Don't look at me that way, Roseanne Roseannadanna.
jokes.setup: Monica giving Phoebe a look about the Mike/David confusion
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's reference to Monica's big hair using SNL character
jokes.explanation: Another hair callback with perfect Phoebe pop culture reference

jokes.text: checking to see if they put chocolates on my pillow yet.
jokes.setup: Ross explaining Joey's interruptions
jokes.punchline: Joey's fixation on hotel chocolates
jokes.explanation: Good character detail about Joey's simple pleasures and priorities

jokes.text: I mean, when I signed up for dino week.. nobody said anything about it being monsoon season.
jokes.setup: Joey complaining about the rain
jokes.punchline: Calling it 'dino week' like summer camp
jokes.explanation: Great Joey-ism reducing paleontology conference to childish 'dino week'

jokes.text: Secret teapot.
jokes.setup: Ross asking about bidding on a teapot
jokes.punchline: Joey's mysterious whisper about a 'secret teapot'
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Joey moment with great economy and mysterious delivery

jokes.text: Well, it didn't say, 'This is a virus.'
jokes.setup: Ross asking why Joey opened a suspicious email
jokes.punchline: Joey's literal logic about virus warnings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey logic showing his literal-minded approach to technology warnings

jokes.text: Nude.. Pictures of Anna Kournikova.
jokes.setup: Admitting what the email said
jokes.punchline: The embarrassingly obvious spam subject line
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment showing Joey's gullibility with obvious spam

jokes.text: Nude pictures of Anna Kournikova? She's never even won a major tournament.
jokes.setup: Joey's admission about the Kournikova spam
jokes.punchline: Charlie's tennis-specific criticism of the choice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character-specific joke showing Charlie's academic approach even to porn spam. Unexpected angle makes it memorable

jokes.text: Well, I tried Billie Jean King, but..
jokes.setup: Charlie's criticism of the tennis player choice
jokes.punchline: Joey's absurd claim he tried other tennis players
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the tennis spam joke with Joey's absurd claim

jokes.text: I can usually make it go away by kissing her.
jokes.setup: Noting Monica and Ross have the same angry look
jokes.punchline: Joey's misguided solution for Ross
jokes.explanation: Good Joey logic suggesting inappropriate solution based on his experience with Monica

jokes.text: I don't think your monologue from Star Wars is gonna help me right now.
jokes.setup: Joey offering a memorized speech for auditions
jokes.punchline: Ross's dismissal of Star Wars as inappropriate for paleontology
jokes.explanation: Solid setup/punchline deflating Joey's unhelpful suggestion

jokes.text: Any chance any of this happened in a galaxy far, far away?
jokes.setup: Ross discussing complex paleontology topics
jokes.punchline: Rachel's Star Wars reference making the science seem fictional
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the Star Wars reference with Rachel's confused perspective on science

jokes.text: I'm sorry, did you say something? I can't hear through all this damned hair!
jokes.setup: Monica trying to hear a phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Her hair growth blocking her hearing
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the hair gag with physical comedy implications

jokes.text: And while I've got you, you've got curly hair. What do you do in the humidity?
jokes.setup: Monica calling Mike about Phoebe
jokes.punchline: Her desperate hair advice question to a stranger
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Monica's priorities even during crisis situations

jokes.text: No matter how much we meddle, we'll never be able to unmeddle.. the thing that you meddled up in the first place!
jokes.setup: Monica and Chandler arguing about their interference
jokes.punchline: Monica's tongue-twisting meddling explanation
jokes.explanation: Wordplay escalation showing Monica's frustration in a characteristically verbose way

jokes.text: Kate Miller it is. And that's the most sex I'm gonna have this weekend.
jokes.setup: Taking on a fake identity at the pharmaceutical convention
jokes.punchline: Rachel's sad admission about her weekend prospects
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor about Rachel's romantic prospects on vacation

jokes.text: I know, I know. I tell you. But on the plus side, we get to wear those white coats.. and stand behind that really tall counter.
jokes.setup: Playing a pharmacist complaining about doctors' handwriting
jokes.punchline: Rachel's shallow focus on the aesthetic perks of pharmacy
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Rachel's fashion-focused perspective even when playing a role

jokes.text: And it's The Days of Our Lives.
jokes.setup: Joey being recognized at the pharmaceutical convention
jokes.punchline: Still adding 'The' to his show title
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation showing Joey hasn't learned from earlier correction

jokes.text: Well, I play the handsome, macho pharmacist.. and Rachel is my student.. who wants to learn all about.. pharmacing.
jokes.setup: Joey improvising a fake movie plot
jokes.punchline: Making up the word 'pharmacing' for pharmacy work
jokes.explanation: Perfect Joey malapropism showing his tendency to make up words and his acting pretensions

jokes.text: My nudity in the film is not tasteful at all.
jokes.setup: Joey describing tasteful nudity in their fake movie
jokes.punchline: Rachel's escalation to completely tasteless nudity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation and contradiction of Joey's 'tasteful' description with perfect economy

jokes.text: Yes, but I did make a pyramid out of the bath products.
jokes.setup: Charlie saying Ross remembered little of his own speech
jokes.punchline: Ross's proud mention of his bathroom pyramid construction
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Ross's quirky priorities and need to contribute something

jokes.text: Oh, my God, I love you.
jokes.setup: Rachel's practical suggestion about saving the champagne cork
jokes.punchline: Ross's emotional overreaction to her frugal tip
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing Ross's appreciation for practical thinking

jokes.text: Actually, it's not that complicated.
jokes.setup: Ross saying his divorce was complicated because his wife was gay
jokes.punchline: His deadpan correction that it's actually simple
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan correction with perfect economy and timing

jokes.text: Hey! High-five!
jokes.setup: Ross and Charlie discovering they both had gay spouses
jokes.punchline: Charlie's enthusiastic celebration of shared trauma
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist moment celebrating shared relationship trauma. Unexpected and memorable

jokes.text: 'Hi. Just dropped by to say your wife's gay.'
jokes.setup: Discussing how friends should have warned them about obvious signs
jokes.punchline: Ross's imagined casual note delivery of devastating news
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist take on how devastating news might be casually delivered. Very quotable

jokes.text: Not enough pills in the world, Rach.
jokes.setup: Rachel noting an old lady was interested in Joey
jokes.punchline: Joey's emphatic rejection using pharmaceutical context
jokes.explanation: Good context-specific joke using pharmaceutical setting for Joey's rejection

jokes.text: No, I'm not blushing.. and I'm sunburned. From, you know, the rain.
jokes.setup: Joey saying Rachel is blushing about liking someone
jokes.punchline: Rachel's absurd claim about getting sunburned from rain
jokes.explanation: Absurd excuse connecting to the rain theme while showing Rachel's flustered state

jokes.text: or Canadian.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asking for local champagne on a budget
jokes.punchline: Adding Canadian as equally cheap/local option
jokes.explanation: Mild geographic humor about wine quality assumptions

jokes.text: It's the humidity!
jokes.setup: Mike being shocked by Monica's appearance
jokes.punchline: Monica's defensive explanation of her massive hair
jokes.explanation: Payoff to the humidity hair running gag with great visual comedy timing

jokes.text: Actually, yeah, that'd be great.
jokes.setup: David sarcastically offering Mike his seat
jokes.punchline: Mike genuinely accepting the offer
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat deflating David's sarcasm with practical acceptance

jokes.text: If it stops raining.
jokes.setup: David wanting to confront Mike outside
jokes.punchline: The practical limitation of the constant rain
jokes.explanation: Callback to rain issue undermining David's dramatic confrontation moment

jokes.text: I wouldn't brag about that, big guy.
jokes.setup: David mentioning he has a ring
jokes.punchline: Mike's dismissive comment about the ring quality
jokes.explanation: Callback to David's pathetic ring with Mike's cutting observation

jokes.text: It was a good trip.
jokes.setup: David listing all the terrible things that happened in Minsk
jokes.punchline: His understated positive assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan understatement after listing career ruin and frostbite. Great David character moment

jokes.text: Hey! Remember when I had corneas?
jokes.setup: The sun finally coming out
jokes.punchline: Ross's complaint about sun damage after humidity hair references
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier sunlight sensitivity joke with Ross experiencing the effects

jokes.text: Not to mention Coldsaurus.
jokes.setup: Ross listing newly discovered dinosaur species
jokes.punchline: Monica's made-up dinosaur name referencing the cold weather
jokes.explanation: Clever portmanteau connecting dinosaur naming conventions to the weather problems

jokes.text: He said 'erectus.'
jokes.setup: Charlie asking about Joey laughing during speech
jokes.punchline: Joey's immature focus on 'erectus' in 'Homo erectus'
jokes.explanation: Juvenile humor perfectly consistent with Joey's character but predictable

jokes.text: Okay, now we're just holding hands.
jokes.setup: Handshake with admiring colleague going on too long
jokes.punchline: Ross's observation about the awkward duration
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about awkwardly long handshakes

jokes.text: Look, I took it too far.
jokes.setup: Charlie's comment about not finding Ross naked in his hotel room
jokes.punchline: Ross's awkward admission he was considering it
jokes.explanation: Cringe admission showing Ross misinterpreting the groupie joke

jokes.text: Not you guys. You've got it going on.
jokes.setup: Making comment about not wanting to see paleontologists shirtless
jokes.punchline: Rachel's quick clarification/flirtation with Ross and Charlie
jokes.explanation: Quick recovery/flirtation moment showing Rachel's social awareness

jokes.text: I punched you. And? Phoebe. And? I clunked your heads together.
jokes.setup: Monica listing what happened last time they played Ping-Pong
jokes.punchline: Escalating violence in competitive playing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Monica competitive escalation with great structure building to violence

jokes.text: This scar? That's from Pictionary.
jokes.setup: Warning Mike about Monica's competitiveness
jokes.punchline: Pictionary causing physical injury
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation turning innocent party game into contact sport. Highly quotable and memorable

jokes.text: Oh, dear God, there's two of them.
jokes.setup: Mike and Monica both showing competitive intensity
jokes.punchline: Rachel's horror at dual competitiveness
jokes.explanation: Good reaction to the competitive escalation between Monica and Mike

jokes.text: Honey, try to focus the trash talk on him.
jokes.setup: Monica insulting the women during competitive play
jokes.punchline: Chandler's gentle redirection of her aggression
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor showing Chandler managing Monica's competitive nature

jokes.text: He was a lawyer.
jokes.setup: Mike citing obscure Ping-Pong rules
jokes.punchline: Monica's explanation for his rule knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good explanation connecting Mike's legal background to his rule-citing behavior

jokes.text: Weather bitch.
jokes.setup: Weather report about beautiful weather everywhere else
jokes.punchline: Joey's personification and insult to the weather
jokes.explanation: Good Joey-ism treating weather as personal enemy with perfect economy

jokes.text: Man, smart people are dull.
jokes.setup: Joey noting Charlie and Ross have more in common
jokes.punchline: His dismissive generalization about intelligent people
jokes.explanation: Character-consistent anti-intellectual comment from Joey after relationship failure

jokes.text: Curious George! You know, the monkey and the guy with the yellow hat. He had a paper route.
jokes.setup: Rachel trying to express how curious she is
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong details about Curious George having a paper route
jokes.explanation: Good Rachel moment showing her tendency to misremember or confuse cultural references

jokes.text: This is the first time in our marriage that I've felt like the more attractive one.
jokes.setup: Monica looking disheveled from competitive Ping-Pong
jokes.punchline: Chandler's admission about usually feeling less attractive
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating relationship humor revealing Chandler's insecurity with perfect timing

jokes.text: I sleep with him!
jokes.setup: Monica defending Chandler during competitive trash talk
jokes.punchline: Her inappropriate personal defense
jokes.explanation: Monica's competitive fury leading to inappropriate personal revelation

jokes.text: Should I use my invisibility to fight crime or for evil?
jokes.setup: Monica and Mike's intense competitive focus making others feel invisible
jokes.punchline: Chandler's philosophical question about his newfound invisibility
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist take on feeling ignored during competition with perfect Chandler sensibility

jokes.text: just nifty.
jokes.setup: Older paleontologist awkwardly hitting on Charlie
jokes.punchline: His dated slang word choice
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing older academic's outdated speech patterns

jokes.text: Well, get out!
jokes.setup: Meeting Jarvis's wife Nancy
jokes.punchline: His shocked/disappointed reaction to his wife appearing
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat showing his disappointment at being caught flirting

jokes.text: I knew that was him!
jokes.setup: Charlie mentioning Joey laughed at 'Homo erectus'
jokes.punchline: Ross's recognition of Joey's juvenile humor
jokes.explanation: Simple callback showing Ross knows Joey's sense of humor

jokes.text: Mike, you win this, you will get the best sex you've ever had in your whole life.
jokes.setup: Phoebe trying to motivate Mike in Ping-Pong
jokes.punchline: Extreme sexual bribery for winning
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phoebe's direct approach to motivation

jokes.text: Well, this is my sickness!
jokes.setup: Chandler referencing marriage vows about sickness and health
jokes.punchline: Monica claiming competitiveness is her 'sickness'
jokes.explanation: Excellent self-aware character moment using marriage vows to justify her competitiveness

jokes.text: You suck. You're welcome, sweetheart.
jokes.setup: Chandler offering to play for Monica after declaring his love
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting her abilities while calling her sweetheart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler contradiction between romantic gesture and brutal honesty

jokes.text: It's like watching porn.
jokes.setup: Watching Chandler's impressive Ping-Pong skills
jokes.punchline: Comparing athletic prowess to pornography
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Phoebe comparison with perfect economy

jokes.text: That's why.
jokes.setup: Monica suggesting they enter doubles tournaments
jokes.punchline: Chandler's confirmation this is why he hid his skills
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing Chandler's foresight about Monica's competitive nature

jokes.text: You know, we, as a group, are not the coolest.
jokes.setup: Paleontologists searching for them in two groups
jokes.punchline: Ross's self-aware observation about paleontologists
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating moment showing Ross's awareness of their social status

tmdb_overview: The gang heads down to Barbados for the paleontology conference. While Ross and Charlie are excited about attending multiple seminars, the rest of the gang just want to enjoy their time on the beach.
